title: euphorbia verruculosa
intro: Euphorbia verruculosa, also known as the rough wax apple or rough spurge, is a species of succulent plant belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of limonoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Euphorbia verruculosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions such as wounds, cuts, and eczema. Euphorbia verruculosa can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
